turns.user_message: I tried going for a long walk this morning hoping the fresh air would shake off this weird fog, but it honestly just felt like moving my legs for no reason. I even put on my favorite playlist while I was out, but the music just sounded like background noise instead of something I could actually connect with. It only started a couple of days ago, and I keep telling myself it will pass once I get some rest. I just expected a little spark of enjoyment to come back, but everything feels completely muted right now.

I decided to bake something from scratch yesterday because I thought the process would finally pull me out of this slump, but I just stared at the ingredients on the counter until they lost all appeal. I kept waiting for that familiar sense of comfort to kick in while I mixed everything together, yet my hands just moved automatically without any real feeling behind them. This has been dragging on for over a week now, and the routine of trying to force a reaction is getting exhausting. I keep hoping the next activity will flip the switch back on, but I am just met with the same heavy blankness every single time.

I spent the whole afternoon trying to recreate that cozy movie night setup I used to love, lighting candles and making snacks, but it just felt like staging a play for an empty room. I sat through the entire film hoping a single scene would finally crack through this numbness, yet I just watched the colors and heard the dialogue without absorbing a single meaningful moment. It has been weeks of this exact same cycle now, and I am completely drained from constantly trying to manufacture joy that refuses to show up. I feel trapped behind thick glass where I can see what happiness looks like, but I can never actually reach it or feel it for myself.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This just started recently.
